title: Nursery Rhyme. CCXLI. Charms.
content:
[Ady, in his 'Candle in the Dark,' 4to, Lond. 1656, p. 59, says that this was a charm to make butter come from the churn. It was to be said thrice.]

        Come, butter, come,
        Come, butter, come!
        Peter stands at the gate,
        Waiting for a butter'd cake;
        Come, butter, come!
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CCXLI. Charms."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
